info.title: SMIFS OrgLens API
info.description:
**SMIFS OrgLens** — Enterprise organizational intelligence platform powered by Darwinbox.

## Overview

Complete API for managing employee data, client records, organizational hierarchy, and third-party integrations.

### External API (v1) — Third-Party Integrations
All `/api/v1/*` endpoints require an **API Key** for external consumption.
- **REST API**: Employee lookups, search, aggregates, org tree, client data, statistics
- **GraphQL API**: `POST /api/v1/graphql` — flexible queries for both Employee and Client data
- **Authentication**: `X-API-Key` header, `Authorization: Api-Key <key>`, or `?api_key=` query param
- **Permissions**: Granular per-key access (employees:read, clients:pii, clients:financial, etc.)
- **Rate Limits**: Configurable per key (default 100 req/min)

### Internal API — Admin Dashboard
All other endpoints require a **JWT Bearer token** obtained via `POST /api/auth/login`.

### Key Features
- **Employee Stack**: 730+ employees synced from Darwinbox with 135+ fields, org tree, field explorer
- **Client Stack**: XLSX import (69 fields), directory, stats, search/filter
- **Mutual Fund Stack**: InvestWell sync, holdings, transactions, SIPs, fund master
- **Backoffice Stack**: SharePro reports, brokerage analysis, unified ledger, trade book
- **Gateway Health**: 10-min cadence probes, outage telemetry, multi-channel alerting
- **360-Degree Security**: Rate limiting, brute-force protection, audit trail, security headers
- **API Usage Analytics**: Granular tracking of all external API access with per-key drill-down
- **RBAC**: Email-based login with 3 roles (superadmin, admin, viewer)

post.description: Authenticate with email and password. Returns JWT token + user info including role.
Set password for a pending_setup account. Used on first login.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile including role.
List all portal users. Superadmin only.

post.description: Create a new user with pending_setup status. Superadmin only. User will set password on first login.

patch.description: Update a user's name or status. Superadmin only. (Role is fixed at superadmin.)

delete.description: Deactivate a user account. Superadmin only.

get.description: Returns server health status. No auth required.
Get the current Darwinbox sync status, total employees, and last sync run details.

post.description: Initiate a Darwinbox data sync. Opens browser, logs in, and begins extraction. May return `awaiting_otp` if OTP is required.
Submit the authenticator OTP to complete Darwinbox login. Only valid when sync status is `awaiting_otp`.
Clear saved Darwinbox browser session. Next sync will require fresh login + OTP.

get.description: Check if a sync checkpoint file exists and how many employees it contains.

post.description: Delete the sync checkpoint file so a fresh sync can start cleanly.
Load the last checkpoint JSON (saved after Darwinbox extraction) and save to MongoDB. No Darwinbox calls needed — uses cached extraction data.

get.description: Paginated employee list with search and filter options. Returns summary fields.

Export all employees and POST them to a target OrgLens instance.

KPI stats: total employees, departments, locations, status breakdown, and last sync info.
New joiners, recent exits, top managers by team size, employee type and designation breakdown.
Monthly joiners/exits trends and headcount snapshots for dashboard charts.

Requires `clients:read`. PII fields need `clients:pii`. Financial fields need `clients:financial`.
Requires `clients:read` + `clients:pii`. Returns the client matching the PAN number.
Requires `clients:read`. Query by UCC, PAN, client_name, state, status, rm_name, risk_profile, branch_code. PII fields need `clients:pii`. Financial fields need `clients:financial`.
Requires `clients:read`. Aggregate counts by status, category, state, risk profile.

One-shot snapshot — master, ledger balance, latest 5 trades, holdings count,
deposit/withdrawal totals, F&O P&L summary. Use this when you only need an
overview; otherwise paginate the granular endpoints.

get.description: Returns all available employee fields organized by category with sensitivity markers.

post.summary: Step 1 of 2 — Parse the XLSX, return columns + auto-mapping + 5-row sample. No DB writes.
Step 2 of 2 — Commit a previewed upload using a (possibly user-edited) field-mapping.

Upsert one client master row.
Bulk upsert client master rows (up to 500 per request). Used for fast full scrapes.
Upsert a sync run row (scraper progress tracking).

Records a 'trigger' marker; the VPS scraper polls this collection
and kicks off a run when it sees a pending trigger.
For MVP we just record it — scraper checks every 30s.

get.description: Called by VPS scraper — returns pending triggers to process.
Branch-grouped CRM overview: client counts + aggregate trading/holding metrics.

Branch-level revenue / cost-to-serve / concentration risk metrics.

Returns for each branch:
  - clients, active_clients, activity_rate
  - revenue (brokerage + additional_brokerage + total_taxes) from bills
  - revenue_per_client, avg_mtm_per_client
  - top3_revenue_share (concentration risk), hhi_revenue (Herfindahl 0-1)
  - trades_per_active_client
Clients within a branch + per-client KPIs (cards).

Aggregated sweep progress for the UI dashboard.

Returns:
  - overall: {pending, running, done, failed, skipped, total, percent}
  - by_fy: [{fy, pending, running, done, failed, percent}]
  - by_report: [{report_code, total, done, failed, percent}]
  - recent_throughput: {done_last_10min, rows_last_10min, avg_seconds_per_job}
  - recent_failures: list of up to 20 recent failed chunks with errors

post.description: Reset failed chunks back to pending so workers re-try them.

Run one batch of client-code backfill. Returns stats.

Call repeatedly (or from a CRON job) until `candidates` = 0.

get.description: Overall completeness of client_code across bo_raw_reports per (report_code, fy).

schema.description: Set to 1 to bypass the 60-s cache

get.description:
Unified chronological ledger for one UCC, filtered to a single Indian FY.
Joins ClientLedgerData ⇄ GetLedgerData on vr_no = voucher.

Response shape:
  {
    ucc, fy, fy_start, fy_end,
    available_fys: [...],         # FYs where either table has rows
    balances: [...],              # GetClientLedgerBalances per segment
    opening:   {runbal, date}?,   # CLD opening row (if present in FY)
    closing:   {runbal, date}?,   # CLD latest row
    totals: {
      cash_cr, cash_dr, cash_net,  # Σ over CLD — cash book movement
      seg_cr,  seg_dr,  seg_net,   # Σ over GLD — segment movement
      by_segment: {NSE: {cr,dr,net,count}, ...},
    },
    timeline: [  # merged by (dt, vr_no), sorted ASC
      { dt, vr_no, type, narr, branch, cheq_no, cheq_dt,
        cash: {cr, dr, runbal},                   # null if absent in CLD
        segments: [ {exchange, cr, dr, dr_cr, voucher_count} ],
        in_cash_book: bool, in_segment_book: bool }
    ],
  }

parameters.name: fy

schema.description: Financial year 'YYYY-YY' (Apr-Mar). Defaults to current FY.
schema.title: Fy

parameters.description: Financial year 'YYYY-YY' (Apr-Mar). Defaults to current FY.

get.description:
Client-wise Brokerage & Charges analysis aggregated from parsed contract
notes (api_cn_charges).  Returns per-FY breakdown (overall + per-exchange
+ month-wise) and reconciles against the legacy `api_brokerage_analysis`
feed (which only updates monthly).
Per-client, FY-filtered trade book aggregated from parsed contract notes.
List the 7 report ids and metadata (used by the frontend menu).

post.description: Manual button trigger (admin JWT). See _merge_client_stack_core.

HMAC-protected EOD bootstrap (called from VPS night script at 22:00 IST).

Steps in order:
  1. Merge new UCCs from MongoDB Client Stack → VPS Postgres queue.
  2. Tier-aware re-queue: per-endpoint TTL drives whether terminal-state
     jobs flip back to ``pending``. Daily endpoints (TTL=24h) requeue
     every night; weekly endpoints (TTL=168h) only when stale.
     Any endpoint with ``force_refresh=true`` in ``bo_sync_endpoint_config``
     is requeued unconditionally and the flag is cleared after the run.
     Endpoints missing from the config table fall back to a 24h TTL.
  3. Skip UCCs whose branch is disabled in ``bo_branch_backfill_config``
     (mark them ``skipped`` so the agent ignores them).

Returns counters so the VPS can log them.

get.description: List endpoint sync config + live queue stats (admin).
Activity probe runs (last 7) + anchor table snapshot.

post.description:
Synchronously refresh GetLedgerData / ClientLedgerData /
GetClientLedgerBalances for one UCC via the VPS relay.

Blocks until SharePro returns and the rows are UPSERTed into Postgres.
Typical latency: ~50 s (ClientLedgerData is the slow upstream call).
Update tier / ttl_hours / force_refresh / cascade_skip for one endpoint.

get.summary: List all branches with client + queue counts and current backfill flag

post.summary: Bulk enable/disable backfill for multiple branches (admin)

get.summary: Brokerage by Branch and RM (current FY)

parameters.name: accyear

schema.description: FY code, e.g., '2627'. Defaults to latest in DB.
schema.title: Accyear

parameters.description: FY code, e.g., '2627'. Defaults to latest in DB.

get.summary: Brokerage totals across all available FYs (multi-year view)
get.description:
Returns one row per FY snapshot we have in the daily-sync tables. Used for
the FY tab in the brokerage widget (year-over-year comparison).

post.summary: Live date-range brokerage analysis (Branch + RM) — Postgres-cached + submit-and-poll
post.description:
Tries the Postgres cache first (instant <50 ms). On miss, kicks off the
upstream BrokerageAnalysis call as a background job (60–90 s) and returns a
job_id for the client to poll via `GET /brokerage/analysis/{job_id}`.
Past-FY windows are cached forever; current windows cached 1 h.

parameters.name: accyear

schema.description: If set, only this FY is cleared
schema.title: Accyear

parameters.description: If set, only this FY is cleared

get.summary: List on-demand documents (CONTRACT|KYC) for a UCC
get.description: Returns metadata only (no base64 payloads). The /file endpoint serves the PDF blob.

post.description: Single probe ingest. Detects up↔down transitions and fires notifications.

get.description: Current state + uptime % over rolling windows + open outage if any (single layer).

Combined snapshot of every monitored layer in one round-trip.
Each layer is queried in parallel from its own pool connection so latency
to Mumbai VPS Postgres is paid once, not N×.

post.description: Fire a synthetic alert through this subscriber so the user can confirm it works.

get.description: List all registered BO modules + their row count + latest scrape timestamp.

Returns the full cross-reference directory with combo histogram.
